phrases.en: In the name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful
phrases.context: Said at the beginning of any significant action: eating, reading, writing, starting a speech or journey; opening every chapter of the Quran
phrases.contextBs: Kaže se na početku svake značajne radnje: jelu, čitanju, pisanju, početku govora ili putovanja; otvara svako poglavlje Kur'ana

phrases.en: All praise is due to Allah
phrases.context: Expressed in gratitude for a blessing, after recovering from illness, completing a task, eating a meal, or when someone asks how you are
phrases.contextBs: Izražava se zahvalnost za blagoslov, nakon oporavka od bolesti, završetka zadatka, obroka, ili kada neko pita kako ste

phrases.context: Said when mentioning any future plan or intention; expresses that all outcomes are in Allah's hands
phrases.contextBs: Kaže se pri spominjanju budućeg plana ili namjere; izražava da su svi ishodi u Allahovim rukama

phrases.en: What Allah has willed (expression of admiration)
phrases.context: Said upon seeing something admirable or impressive — a person's beauty, talent, achievement, or a child's growth; also used to ward off the evil eye
phrases.contextBs: Kaže se kad se vidi nešto divno ili impresivno — nečija ljepota, talenat, postignuće ili rast djeteta; koristi se i za zaštitu od uroka

phrases.en: May Allah reward you with good
phrases.context: Said in gratitude when someone does something kind or helpful for you; considered a more meaningful thanks than شكراً
phrases.contextBs: Kaže se kao zahvalnost kada neko učini nešto lijepo ili korisno za vas; smatra se značajnijom zahvalom od شكراً

phrases.context: A blessing given to someone as thanks, encouragement, or goodwill; suitable after receiving a gift, help, or good news
phrases.contextBs: Blagoslov koji se daje nekome kao zahvalnost, ohrabrenje ili dobronamjernost; prikladno nakon primanja poklona, pomoći ili dobrih vijesti

phrases.context: Said when hearing something sinful, frightening, or shocking; also said before reciting Quran (full form: أعوذ بالله من الشيطان الرجيم)
phrases.contextBs: Kaže se kada se čuje nešto grješno, zastrašujuće ili šokantno; kaže se i prije učenja Kur'ana (puni oblik: أعوذ بالله من الشيطان الرجيم)

phrases.context: Expressed in amazement, wonder, or when witnessing something beautiful in creation; also used to glorify Allah during dhikr
phrases.contextBs: Izražava se u čuđenju ili divljenju, ili pri svjedočenju nečeg lijepog u stvaranju; koristi se i za veličanje Allaha tokom zikra

phrases.context: A supplication (du'a) seeking Allah's forgiveness; said in private prayer, after a sin, or during times of reflection
phrases.contextBs: Dova kojom se traži Allahov oprost; kaže se u privatnoj molitvi, nakon grijeha ili u trenucima razmišljanja

phrases.bs: Neka je Allahov salavat i mir na njega
phrases.en: May Allah's peace and blessings be upon him
phrases.context: Said or written immediately after mentioning the Prophet Muhammad's name; a religious obligation in Islamic etiquette
phrases.contextBs: Kaže se ili piše odmah nakon spomena imena Poslanika Muhammeda; vjerska obaveza u islamskom bontonu

phrases.bs: Neka je Allah s njim zadovoljan
phrases.en: May Allah be pleased with him
phrases.context: Said after the name of a Companion of the Prophet (Sahabi); feminine form: رضي الله عنها; plural: رضي الله عنهم
phrases.contextBs: Kaže se nakon imena Poslanikova ashaba; ženski oblik: رضي الله عنها; množina: رضي الله عنهم

phrases.bs: Uistinu smo Allahovi i Njemu se vraćamo
phrases.en: Indeed, to Allah we belong and to Him we shall return
phrases.context: Recited upon hearing news of death or a serious calamity; a Quranic verse (2:156) expressing submission to Allah's will
phrases.contextBs: Uči se pri vijesti o smrti ili teškoj nesreći; kur'anski ajet (2:156) koji izražava predanost Allahovoj volji
